name: nezam-structured-data-schema
description: JSON-LD implementation, schema.org validation, and rich-snippet targeting aligned to canonical entities.

---

# Purpose

Specify and validate JSON-LD structured data so pages qualify for rich results, AI overviews, and entity graphs. Single-responsibility: schema.org conformance and rich-snippet targeting.

# Inputs

- Entity inventory from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-geo-optimization/SKILL.md`.
- Q&A map from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-aeo-answer-engines/SKILL.md`.
- Page taxonomy and URL hierarchy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-ia-taxonomy/SKILL.md`.
- Brand identity (logo URL, sameAs links) from `docs/core/required/sdd/`.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Map page archetype to schema.org type (`Article`, `Product`, `Service`, `Organization`, `LocalBusiness`, `BreadcrumbList`, `Course`, `Event`, `FAQPage`, `HowTo`).
2. Define a per-archetype JSON-LD template with required + recommended properties; use stable `@id` URIs.
3. Add `BreadcrumbList` for hierarchy and `WebSite` + `SearchAction` for site-level search.
4. Cross-link entities via `sameAs` (Wikipedia, Wikidata, social handles) and `mainEntityOfPage`.
5. Validate with Schema Markup Validator and Rich Results Test before merge.
6. Inject JSON-LD via SSR (avoid client-only rendering); set `Cache-Control` to honor edge.
7. Track rich-result eligibility and impression deltas in Search Console; log in `docs/workspace/context/`.

# Validation & Metrics

- Zero schema validation errors; warnings reviewed and either resolved or documented.
- Every priority page has at least one applicable rich-result type.
- JSON-LD is server-rendered (visible to non-JS crawlers).
- Stable `@id` values across deploys (no hash churn).

# Output Format

- Per-archetype JSON-LD template (`schema/<archetype>.jsonld.template`).
- Validation report (markdown table: page, type, errors, warnings).
- Rich-result eligibility checklist.
- Search Console KPI snapshot.

# Integration Hooks

- `/PLAN seo` produces archetype map.
- `/SCAN seo` runs validation pass.
- `/DEVELOP` consumes JSON-LD templates during page implementation (without writing app code here).
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-geo-optimization/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-aeo-answer-engines/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-serp-feature-targeting/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/sdd-design.mdc](.cursor/rules/sdd-design.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- Hidden or invisible structured data that does not match visible content.
- Single mega-`Article` schema per page when more specific types apply.
- Client-only JSON-LD that crawlers without JS may miss.
- Reusing the same `@id` across different entities.
- Schema spam (e.g., `Review` aggregate without verifiable sources).

# External Reference

- Schema.org core vocabulary (https://schema.org/) — current.
- Google Rich Results: search.google.com/test/rich-results (current).
- Schema Markup Validator: validator.schema.org (current).
- W3C JSON-LD 1.1 (https://www.w3.org/TR/json-ld11/) — current.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: structured-data / json-ld.
